key_insight: CPTabKAN turns EEG features into physiology-guided concepts and learns useful concept interactions, improving mild cognitive impairment detection while remaining interpretable.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a scalable, clinically oriented tabular-learning pipeline that preserves domain structure and exposes which EEG concepts drive decisions.
method: Map 1,379 EEG-derived features into 10 physiologically motivated concept groups.
Apply degree-2 polynomial expansion to capture first- and second-order concept interactions.
Use a Fourier-parameterized TabKAN classifier for nonlinear decision boundaries and concept weighting.
evidence_signal: On the Study of Osteoporotic Fractures cohort (372 subjects), CPTabKAN-Second Order achieved a weighted F1-score of 0.9038 under 10-fold cross-validation, outperforming GradientBoosting by 5.65 percentage points.
watch_item: Results come from a single cohort and EEG/PSG feature pipeline, so external validation and implementation robustness across sites, sensors, and preprocessing choices remain important.
paper_title: Interpretable Concept-Guided Polynomial Tabular Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for EEG-Based Mild Cognitive Impairment Detection
